message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1, fixes applied vs main). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
The file /tmp/pass1_confirm.json is a JSON array of 22 pass-1 findings (each: issue, title, file, sev, problem, suggested_fix).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #1 of 2 for finding INDEX 8 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_confirm.json.
Read that item (its issue number, problem, suggested_fix, file). Then VERIFY THE APPLIED FIX on the current branch: read the changed source AND its regression test (git diff vs main, or just read the file + tests), and where useful run a PoC against the venv to confirm the original problem is actually resolved.
Return: appropriate = the fix correctly addresses the root cause in a sound way (not a hack/incomplete patch/over-claim); complete = the fix fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would catch a regression. Set either false if not, and say exactly what is missing. Echo the issue number in 'issue'.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify the applied fix for finding INDEX 8. Let me start by reading the finding.

content.content:
{
  "issue": "109",
  "title": "A raised run leaves a partial provenance trail with no failure entry \u2014 the abort reason is unrecoverable from the trail",
  "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py",
  "sev": "MEDIUM",
  "problem": "GOVERNANCE.md \u00a73 and the docstring at lines 102-103 explicitly promise that \"a caller may pass its own trail to run_study so a run that *raises* (a blocked gate, a firewall violation) still leaves an auditable partial trail.\" That partial trail is auditable for what *succeeded*, but it records NOTHING about the failure itself: when any phase raises (IngestionError, FirewallViolation, PreRegistrationError, GateError, or an adapter exception), control leaves through the bare try/finally at lines 269/522 and the finally block only calls tracker.end_run \u2014 it never records a provenance entry for th",
  "suggested_fix": "Wrap the phase machine body in try/except/finally: on any exception, before re-raising, record a terminal entry e.g. trail.record('run_failed', f'{type(exc).__name__} during {visited[-1].name if visited else \"start\"}', error_type=type(exc).__name__, message=str(exc), last_phase=visited[-1].name if visited else None). Keep it inside the chain (so verify_records still passes) and guard the record() "
}

@@ -79,6 +87,10 @@ class GateReview:

     payload: Mapping[str, Any]
 
+    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
+        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
+        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
+
 
 GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
 
@@ -266,6 +278,13 @@ def run_study(
         except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
             trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
 
+    # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
+    # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122). Best-effort.
+    bootstrap_tracing()
+    corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
+    ok = False

         trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
+        ok = True
+    except Exception as exc:
+        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
+        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
+        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
+        try:
+            trail.record(

+        raise

-                tracker.end_run(run_id)
+                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
+                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
                 pass

content.text: The fix for #109 is present. Let me check `visited` is populated correctly before the failure point, and look at the regression test.

+def test_failed_run_records_failure_entry_and_marks_tracker_failed(tmp_path):
+    # #109 + #110: a raised run leaves a 'run_failed' provenance entry AND ends the tracker run
+    # as FAILED (distinguishable from success).
+    from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail
+
+    class T:
+        def __init__(self):
+            self.ended = None
+
+        def start_run(self, name, params):
+            return "r"
+
+        def log_metric(self, *a):
+            pass
+
+        def log_artifact(self, *a):
+            pass
+
+        def end_run(self, run_id, status="FINISHED"):
+            self.ended = status
+
+    def reject(r):
+        return GateDecision(approved=False, gate=r.gate, reason="halt")
+
+    src = ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json")
+    trail = ProvenanceTrail()
+    t = T()
+    with pytest.raises(GateError):
+        run_study(ref.make_plan(src, modes=DISCOVERY), gate_handler=reject, tracker=t, trail=trail)
+    assert t.ended == "FAILED"
+    failed = [e for e in trail.entries if e.kind == "run_failed"]
+    assert failed and failed[0].payload["error_type"] == "GateError"
+
+
 def test_feature_builders_are_computed_and_recorded(tmp_path):
     from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
 
@@ -476,7 +510,7 @@ def test_tracker_start_and_end_failures_are_swallowed(tmp_path):
         def log_artifact(self, *a):
             pass
 
-        def end_run(self, run_id):
+        def end_run(self, run_id, status="FINISHED"):
             raise RuntimeError("end boom")
 
     # start_run failing -> run_id stays None, run still completes; end_run failure swallowed

content.text: The API matches: `trail.entries`, `e.kind`, `e.payload`. The `run_failed` record stays within the chain (recorded via `trail.record` before re-raise), so `verify_records` would still pass since it's the last appended entry. Note the failure entry is recorded AFTER the `trail.verify()` at line 538 only when the failure is the verify itself; for gate failures it's recorded mid-chain which is fine. Let me run the regression test plus the full pipeline test to confirm no regressions.

content.text: The test passes the `trail` explicitly (caller-owned) and asserts `run_failed` exists with `error_type == "GateError"` — directly matching the finding's promise that a raised run leaves an auditable failure entry in the caller's trail. The test also asserts tracker status FAILED (#110, separate finding). Let me do a quick negative-control PoC to confirm the assertion is non-trivial (would catch a regression).

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python - <<'EOF'
import tempfile, pathlib
from tests import reference_study as ref
from assay_engine.pipeline import run_study, GateDecision
from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateError
from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail
from assay_engine.contracts.modes import StudyMode

d = pathlib.Path(tempfile.mkdtemp())
src = ref.write_source(d/"c.json")
trail = ProvenanceTrail()
def reject(r): return GateDecision(approved=False, gate=r.gate, reason="halt")
try:
    run_study(ref.make_plan(src, modes={StudyMode.DISCOVERY}), gate_handler=reject, trail=trail)
except GateError:
    pass
failed = [e for e in trail.entries if e.kind=="run_failed"]
print("run_failed entries:", len(failed))
if failed:
    print("payload:", dict(failed[0].payload))
    print("last entry kind:", trail.entries[-1].kind)
# verify chain still intact AFTER the failure entry
trail.verify()
print("trail.verify() OK -> failure entry is inside the intact chain")
EOF
input.description: PoC confirming run_failed entry recorded inside intact chain

content.text:
The PoC confirms the fix works end-to-end: a gate-blocked run now leaves a `run_failed` entry recording `error_type`, `message`, and `last_phase`, and the entry is inside the intact provenance chain (`trail.verify()` passes).

One consideration: the finding mentions the failure entry should cover all phase exceptions including the final `trail.verify()` at line 538. Since `trail.verify()` is inside the `try`, if verify itself raises, the except handler calls `trail.record("run_failed", ...)` which appends a new entry — but verify already failed because the chain was broken, so the record may add to an already-broken chain. This is an edge case (chain tampering), not the primary scenario (gate/firewall/ingestion aborts), and the `record` is guarded by try/except. The fix is sound for the documented scenarios in the finding.

input.reasoning:
The fix correctly addresses the root cause. In src/assay_engine/pipeline.py the phase machine body (lines 288-539) is now wrapped in try/except/finally. On any exception, before re-raising, it records a terminal provenance entry: trail.record("run_failed", f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}", error_type=..., message=str(exc)[:2000], last_phase=...). last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start" matches the suggested_fix exactly. The record() call is guarded by an inner try/except so it never masks the original failure, and the entry is recorded via the same trail.record() chain so verify_records still passes (confirmed by PoC: trail.verify() succeeds with the run_failed entry as the last link). This is the precise approach the finding requested — not a hack or over-claim. The message is bounded to 2000 chars (sensible). The except re-raises, preserving the original exception/traceback via bare raise.

complete=true: the finding (a raised run leaves no failure entry, abort reason unrecoverable) is fully resolved — the abort reason (error_type, message, last_phase) is now recoverable from the caller-owned trail for the documented abort paths (GateError, FirewallViolation, IngestionError, PreRegistrationError, adapter exceptions, all of which raise out of the phase body). A regression test exists and would catch a regression: tests/test_pipeline.py::test_failed_run_records_failure_entry_and_marks_tracker_failed passes its own ProvenanceTrail, triggers a gate rejection (GateError), and asserts both that a run_failed entry exists and that failed[0].payload["error_type"] == "GateError". Removing the except-block record would make this assertion fail. Test passes against the venv (32/32 pipeline tests green), and my independent PoC reproduced the run_failed entry with correct payload inside an intact chain.

Minor non-blocking edge case: if the final trail.verify() (line 538) itself raises due to upstream chain tampering, the except handler appends run_failed to an already-broken chain; but this is outside the finding's documented scenarios (phase aborts) and the record is guarded, so it does not reduce the fix's correctness or completeness for the finding.
input.evidence: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:541-557 (except Exception: records run_failed with error_type/message/last_phase before re-raise); pipeline.py:228-233 (visited populated by enter()); pipeline.py:558-560 (finally: trace_ctx.close + tracker.end_run status). Regression test: tests/test_pipeline.py:331-364 (test_failed_run_records_failure_entry_and_marks_tracker_failed) asserts run_failed entry with payload error_type==GateError. Provenance API: src/assay_engine/provenance.py:191 (entries), :58-60 (kind/payload), :202 (verify). PoC output: run_failed entries=1, payload={'error_type':'GateError','message':"gate 'review-locked-hypotheses' blocked PREREGISTER->CONFIRM: halt",'last_phase':'PREREGISTER'}, trail.verify() OK. pytest: 32 passed.
